openEnded.frustration: The first glaring issue was the lack of viable departure and arrival airports, as well as usable dates. While the hint system does seem to trigger after several dead ends or searches with no results, the hints themselves are so vague that they do not offer much real help. At best, they loosely confirm valid or invalid routes, but the ambiguity makes it difficult to tell whether that is intentional or simply unhelpful.
openEnded.ease: Using the dropdown menus and date pickers was easy enough. That said, finding a viable itinerary was next to impossible. Overall, it felt like a real chore.
openEnded.confusion: I understand the intent of the study, but I struggle to see how this system translates to a real world use case. I rarely have trouble finding flights using services like Expedia or Priceline, so I do not understand how System A is meant to function in a similar way to its real world counterparts.
openEnded.strategy: At first, I approached the tasks methodically. After running into repeated failures, I began doing more random searches. Eventually, I had to rely on memory, using previously successful itinerary selections as a starting point for later searches.
openEnded.improvement: I do not think I can answer this question accurately, as my understanding of how itinerary searches are supposed to work within this system still feels incomplete after participating in the study. That said, even if the study itself has merit, I do not see how it could realistically influence the broader industry.

openEnded.frustration: Compared to System A, I would say that System B was largely a success. There was very little guesswork involved, if any at all. Finding viable itineraries was easy.
openEnded.ease: Most notably, not having to guess or deduce viable dates for each trip type, which was a major issue in System A.
openEnded.confusion: There were two airports listed as departure options under the Round-Trip element, but only one of them was actually viable. This seemed more like a system glitch than anything else. While I was able to select the non viable departure airport, I could not then select an arrival airport. As a result, I was effectively left with only one real option.
openEnded.strategy: Honestly, this system was much easier to use than System A. There was no real strategy involved beyond simply using the system as intended, and I did not have to think much about anything.
openEnded.improvement: If System B works the way I believe it does, I would ensure that only viable departure and arrival airports are presented as selectable options. What happened under the Round-Trip element should not occur at all.
